data.title: Valereum Signs Digital Asset MoU with US-based Blubird
data.description:
Valereum (VLRM.AQ) signed a digital asset-focused memorandum of understanding with US-based software-as-a-service group Blubird Global, according to a Friday release.

The financial technology company will secure a 10-year license to Blubird's Web3 Founders Tools suite. The parties also plan to initially list up to $2 billion of Blubird's more than $55 billion in tokenized assets on Valereum's marketplace, subject to issuer consent as well as technical and regulatory clearances.

The companies may also pursue potential joint initiatives as Valereum looks to expand its digital asset ecosystem and advance its monetization strategy and global growth.

data.title: Mipco Seamless Rings (Gujarat) Gets Board Nod for Name Change
data.description:
Mipco Seamless Rings (Gujarat) has received board approval to change the company's name to Mipco, according to a Thursday filing to the BSE.

The proposal is subject to the approval of the members and the applicable regulatory authorities.

data.title: Polish CPI in May lower than expected, boosts rate cut bets
data.description:
Inflation in Poland was slightly lower than expected in May, official data showed on Friday, strengthening market expectations that the central bank's Monetary Policy Council (MPC) will cut interest rates, possibly in July.

Annual inflation in May was 4.1%, according to a flash estimate from the statistics office, just below the 4.2% forecast in a Reuters poll, and easing from 4.3% in March. The central bank's inflation target is 1.5-3.5%

Month-on-month inflation fell by 0.2% in April, while analysts had expected an increase of 0.1%.

"The data, combined with the reduction in gas tariffs, confirms that CPI inflation will fall to around 3% - a return to the broad inflation target with a bang. Our forecast of a 25 basis point rate cut in July remains valid," PKO BP analysts wrote in a commentary on the data.

Poland's energy market regulator has cut the gas prices that wholesaler PGNiG Obrot Detaliczny charges to households and vulnerable users including hospitals and schools by 14.8%.

Finance minister Andrzej Domanski wrote on social media platform X that lower tariffs "will lower the inflation path in the coming months".

The MPC lowered the cost of credit by 50 basis points in May, making its first cut since October 2023, citing slowing inflation, weakening wage growth and worsening economic conditions. The main interest rate is currently 5.25%.

National Bank of Poland Governor Adam Glapinski said earlier this month that the fact that the bank had cut rates did not mean a cycle of monetary policy easing was beginning.

Some members of the MPC indicated that July, when new inflation forecasts will be published, may be a good time to start discussions on further interest rate cuts, which could take place later this year.

data.title: Japan's Housing Starts Fall 27% in April
data.description:
Japan's housing starts fell nearly 27% year over year in April after a 39% increase the month prior, the Ministry of Land, Infrastructure, Transport, and Tourism reported Friday.

Construction starts of owned houses dropped 24% after a 37% gain a month earlier. Construction starts of rented homes fell nearly 28% to reverse a 51% increase in March.

Meanwhile, construction starts of issued homes soared 27% in April, but significantly lower than the over 99% gain in the month prior.

Built-for-sale construction starts gained close to 30%, while prefabricated starts fell 21%. Two-by-four housing starts decreased 47%.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Markets Headlines at 5 AM ET: Global Markets Mixed Amid Tariff Uncertainty; PCE Inflation Data Eyed  | Trumpâ€™s ...
data.description:
Global Markets Mixed Amid Tariff Uncertainty; PCE Inflation Data Eyed

U.S. stock futures were little changed Friday ahead of key inflation data, while shares in Asia ended lower and European bourses were mixed.

----

Trump's Team Plots Plan B for Imposing Tariffs

If a court appeal fails, the president's advisers are considering a stopgap tariff regime.

----

Falling Spanish Inflation Readies ECB for Rate Cut

Cooling inflation in Spain and France is putting the European Central Bank on course to reach its inflation target, cementing expectations of another rate cut.

----

Rising Inflation, Weak Production Put Bank of Japan in Tough Spot

Japanese government data released Friday present a mixed picture of rising inflation and sluggish industrial activity.

----

U.S.-China trade talks â€˜stalled,' may require direct talks between Trump and Xi, Bessent says

Trade talks between the U.S. and China are "a bit stalled," Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Thursday, adding that a breakthrough will likely hinge on President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping speaking directly.

----

Bank of America CEO Brian Moynihan on What Makes the U.S. â€˜Fundamentally Strong'

Moynihan recently sat down for a discussion as part of the At Barron's interview series.

----

The Stock Market Is Crushing an Old Wall Street Cliche

If you sold this May, you missed a historic surge.

----

Trump Tells Powell He Is Making a Mistake by Not Cutting Interest Rates

The president and the Federal Reserve chair meet for the first time in Trump's second term.

----

Inflation Likely Cooled in April. Don't Expect It to Last.

Forecasts for the PCE, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation measure, suggest that prices moderated last month.

----

Nvidia's Climb Carries Stocks Through Trade Reversals

Earnings helped lift the chip maker's shares 3.2% Thursday.

----

U.S. GDP Shrank Less Than First Reported Last Quarter. This Is Why.

The Bureau of Economic Analysis released its second estimate for first-quarter GDP growth Thursday morning.

----

Pending Home Sales Slump as Mortgage Prices Weigh

The number of homes going under contract in the U.S. fell sharply in April, as high mortgage prices continue to hamper the market, according to a monthly index.

----

Returning to More Open Trade Calls for Hard Work, Says BOE's Bailey

The head of the Bank of England says strains in the global trading system that have most recently been manifested in higher U.S. tariffs make life difficult for central bankers.

----

U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Fall More Than Expected

U.S. crude oil inventories fell by 2.8 million barrels, exceeding estimates for a drop of 600,000 barrels.

data.title: Dow Jones Top Company Headlines at 5 AM ET: Out of DOGE, Elon Musk Returns to His Bruised Business Empire | EssilorLuxottica ...
data.description:
Out of DOGE, Elon Musk Returns to His Bruised Business Empire

Elon Musk is ready to get obsessed with his companies again. There's a lot for him to contend with at Tesla, SpaceX and xAI.

----

EssilorLuxottica to Buy Optegra in Medical AI Push

The eyewear group said the deal further expands its reach beyond Ray-Bans, frames and lenses into AI-powered technologies and science-backed eyecare solutions.

----

Nippon Steel to Invest $6 Billion to Cut Carbon Emissions

The Japanese steelmaker said the investment in electric arc furnaces will increase production capacity while significantly reducing emissions.

----

Costco to Rely on Advancing Orders, Production Shifts to Offset Tariffs

The warehouse-club chain is taking steps to reduce its exposure to tariffs by pulling orders forward and moving sourcing of private-label products to the regions where they are sold.

----

Target at the Tipping Point: What It Needs to Do Now

The storied company is in a dicey place, plagued by empty shelves, skimpy staff, and messy stores. It's time for drastic action.

----

SEC Dismisses Lawsuit Against Binance

The regulatory agency has been winding down Biden-era enforcement actions against crypto firms during the Trump administration.

----

Dell Technologies Raises Outlook as Sales Rise on AI Demand

The maker of computers and software on Thursday said it now expects adjusted earnings per share of $9.40, up from its previous outlook for $9.30.

----

Nvidia's Most Impressive Feat Yet? How the Chip Maker Overcame a China Ban.

Nvidia earnings don't do its quarter justice. The chip maker handled the loss of a massive business line and still generated impressive growth.

----

Microchip Technology Narrows Outlook on Higher Bookings

Microchip is performing better than management expected when it hosted its investor call on May 8, with higher-than-expected bookings, Chief Executive Steve Sanghi said.

----

Gap sees sales stalling, and puts a number on the extra cost of tariffs

Gap Inc.'s stock drops after the retailer called for flat current-quarter sales.

----

Berkshire Hathaway Stock Is Slipping. The â€˜Buffett Premium' Is Leaking Out.

Warren Buffett surprised investors at the May 3 Berkshire Hathaway annual meeting with plans to give up the CEO job at the end of 2025.

----

Ulta Raises Full-Year Outlook as Consumers Keep Spending on Beauty

Same-store sales increased 2.9%, beating the 0.2% bump Wall Street was anticipating. An increase in average ticket and the number of transactions helped drive same-store-sales growth, Ulta said.

----

Boeing Stock Jumps on CEO Comments. Here's What He Said.

Boeing CEO Kelly Ortberg has positive news about the production rates, 737 MAX variants, and China. Investors look pleased.

----

Ben & Jerry's Calls Gaza Conflict a Genocide, Putting Unilever In A Tough Spot

Ben & Jerry's independent board plans to post a statement expressing solidarity with protesters against deaths of Palestinians in Gaza.

----

Retailers, Ducking Trade-War Curveballs, Stick to Their Plans

As legal rulings roll in on Trump's tariff policies, retail executives say they have shifted their supply chains and many price increases already have hit shelves.

data.title: Brödernas publishes quarterly report for Jan-Mar 2025 and defers interest payment due in June 2025
data.description:
Brödernas Group AB

May 30, 2025, 11.15 CET

Reference is made to Brödernas Group AB (publ) ("Brödernas"), SEK 175,000,000 senior secured callable fixed rate bonds with ISIN NO0013250597 ("Existing Bonds").

Brödernas hereby publishes the report for the first quarter (Q1) for the period 1 January - 31 March. The report is available as an attached document and on the company's website (investors.brodernas.nu).

1 January - 31 March 2025

Net sales amounted to SEK 107m (112m)

EBITDA amounted to SEK -5m (6m)

Pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Existing Bonds, Brödernas shall ensure that net leverage ratio (net interest bearing debt to EBITDA) does not exceed 6.00x. For the reference period ending 31 March 2025, net leverage ratio (calculated in accordance with the terms and conditions for the bonds) is in excess of 6.00x.

In addition to what's previously being stated, and due to the fact that Brödernas Group AB (publ) is in corporate restructuring proceedings and there are too many uncertainties in the organization to be able to determine the correct financial figures, the publication of the quarterly report does not contain a balance sheet or cash flow analysis, meaning that an event of default has occurred under the bond terms.

Further, Brödernas has, in accordance with Section 12 of the terms and conditions for the Existing Bonds, an upcoming payment of inter alia cash interest in amount of approximately SEK 7,000,000 due on the Interest Payment Date 10 June 2025. Due to the ongoing restructuring process and the group's liquidity situation, Brödernas will defer the interest payment due 10 June 2025 under the Existing Bonds until further notice.

For further information, please contact:

Richard Forsshéll, CEO

press@brodernas.se

This information is information that Brödernas Group AB is obliged to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation. The information was submitted, through the provision of the specified contact person, for publication on 30 May 2025 at 11.15 CET.

https://news.cision.com/brodernas-group-ab/r/brodernas-publishes-quarterly-report-for-jan-mar-2025-and-defers-interest-payment-due-in-june-2025%2Cc4157174

Financial Report Q1 2025 - Brödernas Group AB

data.title: OncoZenge Extends Deadline for Chinese Investment Deal
data.description:
OncoZenge said Friday it extended the deadline for regulatory clearance of the investment deal with China's Sichuan-Yangtian Biopharmaceuticals to June 30.

The Swedish oral pain medicine company said the move follows the longer-than-expected lead time for overseas direct Investment processes in China. The filing is currently under review by the provincial department of commerce.

The agreement, announced in January, will fund the phase 3 trial for BupiZenge in Europe.

data.title: Truecaller's board has decided to resume share buybacks
data.description:
Truecaller AB

The Board of Truecaller has resolved to utilize the mandate adopted by the Annual General Meeting on the 23rd of May 2025 to buy back B-shares. The purpose of the buybacks is to give the board of directors the opportunity to continuously adapt the company's capital structure and thereby contribute to increased shareholder value, to be able to exploit attractive acquisition opportunities by fully or partly financing future acquisitions of companies, with the company's own shares, and for financing and/or securing the delivery of series B shares in long-term incentive programs approved by the general meeting.

The program will be initiated at the earliest today the 30th of May and will last until the 2026 Annual General Meeting at the latest.

Reporting of repurchased shares will be carried out in accordance with applicable rules.

The repurchase program is subject to the following terms:

  • The share repurchases shall be made on Nasdaq Stockholm in accordance with its Rulebook for Issuers of Shares (below the “Rulebook”)
  • The share repurchases shall commence no earlier than today the 30th of May 2025, and shall end no later than the 2026 Annual General Meeting
  • The share repurchases are to be carried out on one or more occasions, at a price per share within the registered span of share prices on the stock exchange at the particular time, (i.e. the interval between the highest buying price and the lowest selling price) on Nasdaq Stockholm
  • The share repurchases may only be made in accordance with the restrictions regarding volume for acquisitions of own shares stated in the Rulebook
  • The total number of series B shares that may be repurchased may correspond to a maximum of 10 percent of the total number of all outstanding shares.
  • The shares are to be paid for in cash

The total number of shares in Truecaller is today 353,079,080 shares, of which 46,783,800 A shares and 301,277,494 B shares and 5,017,786 C shares. All C shares have been repurchased by Truecaller to secure the company's commitments in incentive programs decided by the AGM.

As of today Truecaller holds 3,945,332 B shares and 5,017,786 C shares in treasury.

For more information, please contact:

Andreas Frid, Head of IR & Communication

+46 705 29 08 00

andreas.frid@truecaller.com

About Truecaller:

Truecaller (TRUE B) is the leading global platform for verifying contacts and blocking unwanted communication. We enable safe and relevant conversations between people and make it efficient for businesses to connect with consumers. Fraud and unwanted communication are endemic to digital economies. especially in emerging markets. We are on a mission to build trust in communication. Truecaller is an essential part of everyday communication for more than 450 million active users. Truecaller is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm since 8 October 2021. For more information please visit corporate.truecaller.com

https://news.cision.com/truecaller-ab/r/truecaller-s-board-has-decided-to-resume-share-buybacks%2Cc4157195

Share repurchases 250530

data.title: Greek Producer Inflation Eases to 3-Month Low
data.description:
The annual producer prices index in Greece slowed to 0.5% in April 2025 from a more than two-year high of 2.1% gain in the previous month.

The latest figure marked the lowest reading since January, as prices moderated for energy (1.4% vs 5.2% in March) and intermediate goods (2.5% vs 3.7%).

In addition, deflation worsened for non-durable consumer goods (-0.6% vs -0.4%).

Meanwhile, costs accelerated for both durable consumer goods (4.6% vs 4.3%) and capital goods (3.1% vs 2.7%).

On a monthly basis, producer prices dropped by 2.8% in April 2025, following a 1.7% fall in the previous month.

data.title: Craig-Hallum Adjusts Price Target on NVIDIA to $195 From $175, Maintains Buy Rating

data.title: So-Young to Change ADS Ratio to 1 ADS Representing 15 Shares From 13 ADSs Representing 10 Shares >SY
Greek retail sales rise 0.3% y/y in March
data.description:
Greek retail sales by volume rose 0.3% in March compared to the same month a year earlier, after an upwardly revised 5.6% rise in February, statistics service ELSTAT said on Friday.

Sales volume of the food sector was down 0.3% year-on-year, with the sales volume of the non-food sector (except automotive fuel) increasing 3.4% while the automotive fuel sector's sales volume was up 0.5% on the year, the data showed.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Initiates Insulet at Buy With $380 Price Target
data.description: Insulet has an average rating of overweight and mean price target of $338.77, according to analysts polled by FactSet.

data.title: Dell rises after lifting annual profit forecast
data.description:
** Shares of Dell Technologies rise 1.91% to $115.50 in premarket trading

** Co raises annual profit forecast, expects annual adj profit to be $9.40 per share, compared with its prior forecast of $9.30

** "Looking long-term, Dell is well positioned to lead the industry both in relation to AI deployments with its differentiated capabilities, as well as relative to its traditional IT portfolio supported by best-in class supply chain management" - J.P.Morgan

** JPM raises DELL PT to $125 from $111, an upside of ~10% to the stock's last close of $113.63

** Softness in macro impacting higher margin product lines remains a source of concern in near-term - JPM

** Avg rating of 24 brokerages is "buy"; their median PT is $128 - data compiled by LSEG

** Through Thursday, DELL stock down ~1% YTD

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0904 GMT - Sterling faces some temporary selling pressure as investors rebalance portfolios for month-end, Convera strategist George Vessey says in a note. Sterling's strong month-to-date performance makes it vulnerable to these month-end flows, he says. However, sterling could soon resume its earlier rally, potentially rising to $1.40 before year-end, he says. This assumes the "sell America trade" continues, supported by eroding confidence in U.S. policymaking and a deteriorating U.S. economic outlook. Such an appreciation would also require a resilient U.K. economy and the Bank of England remaining cautious about cutting interest rates. Sterling falls 0.1% to $1.3479, having reached a three-year high of $1.3594 Monday, according to FactSet. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0855 GMT - Sanofi shares fall in European morning trading, while Regeneron Pharmaceuticals is sharply lower in U.S. premarket trading, after the companies reported mixed results from two late-stage trials of a respiratory drug they are jointly developing. Sanofi and Regeneron said drug candidate Itepekimab missed the key goal of a phase 3 trial, but met the primary objective in the other study. Paris-listed shares in Sanofi fall 5.8%, while Regeneron shares drop 12% in U.S. premarket trading. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0821 GMT - Reckitt Benckiser's work to refocus its portfolio over the past few years is starting to pay off, UBS's Guillaume Delmas and Silvia Xu say in a research note. The U.K. consumer-goods company accelerated its efforts after a strategy update and is now past the inflection point and starting to achieve organic sales growth above the industry average, UBS says. The company behind brands such as Lysol, Dettol and Strepsils is seeking to focus on a concentrated portfolio of core brands that are leaders in their categories and have significant opportunities for greater penetration and more premium offerings, UBS says. Shares are up 1.9% at 50.40 pounds. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0758 GMT - Kuaishou Technology's outlook looks positive in 2Q with steady core businesses and encouraging AI progress, according to Deutsche Bank Research analyst Leo Chiang in commentary. Kling AI, a tool for creating imaginative images and videos, continued to gain traction, generating 150 million yuan in revenue in the first quarter, Chiang says. This progress is likely to continue, he adds. The analyst expects its core ads and e-commerce businesses to maintain steady growth in 2Q as it continues to enhance its video-content ecosystem and monetization capabilities. DB keeps a buy rating, with a target price of HK$75.00. Shares were last traded at HK$53.00. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0718 GMT - Bajaj Auto's business focus is likely to offer sufficient operational levers to sustain margins despite rising input costs, Axis Securities' Shridhar Kallani says in a research report. This focus includes expansion of its portfolio of premium motorcycles, electric two-wheelers and electric three-wheelers, and leveraging of its new product launches, the analyst notes. For FY 2026, management is seeking to gain leadership in the 125cc+ motorcycle segment, scale key two- and three-wheeler platforms and increase exports, particularly in Latin America, the analyst adds. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to INR9,890.00 from INR9,380.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 2.5% lower at INR8,652.60. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0714 GMT - Chocoladefabriken Lindt & Spruengli stood out during past six months, when record cocoa prices and a challenging consumer backdrop posed the ultimate test for chocolate brands, Morgan Stanley analysts say in a research note. A strong share performance by the Swiss chocolatier in the year to date left many investors wondering about the prospects for the stock, Morgan Stanley says. However, the past six months proved the robustness of Lindt's brands and business model and consensus expectations on Lindt's earnings face limited risks, the analysts say. "Its ability to defend its FY25 guidance is also attractive in the current backdrop where corporate earning pressures have increased, and cocoa market risks are also rolling over," the analysts say. Shares rise 1.4% and are up 29% since the start of 2025. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0712 GMT - M&G's partnership with Japanese life insurance company Dai-ichi Life is very positive news for the U.K. investment management firm and will boost its shares, J.P. Morgan analysts say. The deal is a large strategic commitment from Japan's largest listed life insurer, they note. Dai-ichi Life's decision to acquire a significant stake in M&G shows a high level of confidence in the execution of the London-listed company's strategy. The two partners could also develop new products together, the analysts add. Shares in M&G are up 8% at 2.42 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0647 GMT - SIA Engineering stands to benefit from new comprehensive services agreements signed with Singapore Airlines and Scoot recently, OCBC Investment Research's Ada Lim says in a research report. Based on the maintenance, repair, and overhaul services provider's announcement on the Singapore Exchange, the agreements are expected to generate a total labor revenue of S$1.3 billion over two years. This translates to a significant labor revenue step-up of 54.7% per annum when compared to the previous agreements, the analyst estimates. OCBC updates its model to incorporate the new contracts, and raises the stock's fair value estimate to S$3.00 from S$2.64 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are 0.4% lower at S$2.71. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0632 GMT - News that Swiss eyecare specialist Alcon received its first U.S. Food and Drug Administration approval since its listing in 2019 is slightly positive, but comes as little surprise, Vontobel's Sibylle Bischofberger says in a research note. The drug, Tryptyr, was approved to treat the signs and symptoms of dry eye disease, a condition that affects around 38 million people in the U.S. and for which there is a need for new treatment options, the analyst says. "We expect Alcon to continue performing well, especially with the launch of several products in 2025. However, results may remain somewhat volatile due to challenging market conditions," Vontobel says. Zurich-listed shares closed 1.95% lower Wednesday at 70.56 Swiss francs, with markets in Switzerland closed Thursday due to a holiday. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)Nestle's shares closed 1.35% lower at 87.51 francs on Wednesday, with markets in Switzerland closed Thursday for a holiday. "Nestle's Organic Growth Likely to Be Offset by Swiss Franc Strength — Market Talk," at 0603 GMT, misstated the day.

0607 GMT - Samvardhana Motherson International may benefit from its entry into new segments, Nomura analysts say in a research report, as they raise the stock's target price to INR170.00 from INR155.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Management has commented that the automotive component supplier will target new solutions like components for semiconductor machines and printed circuit board assemblies for autos, the analysts note. Management also said the Indian company will seek to continue ramping up production of consumer electronics. The brokerage raises its FY 2026-2027 revenue estimates for the company by 3%-5% to reflect a stronger EUR/INR, partly offset by lower organic growth assumptions. Shares are 1.7% higher at INR154.75. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0603 GMT - Nestle is facing rising currency headwinds, with recent strength in the Swiss franc relative to the dollar likely to offset the company's organic sales growth, Berenberg analysts say in a research note. "Since the start of 2025, the Swiss franc has strengthened by 10% versus the U.S. dollar, and our model suggests the currency translation effect to Nestle's group sales in 2025 will be [minus] 4.2%, more than offsetting organic sales growth of 3.4%," the analysts say. Currency headwinds are set to hurt the Swiss food company's profitability as well, they add. Berenberg cuts its target price on Nestle to 87 francs from 90 francs. Shares closed 1.35% lower at 87.51 francs on Wednesday with markets closed Thursday for a holiday, and are up nearly 17% since the start of 2025. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com) Corrections & Amplifications

This market talk item was corrected at 0633 GMT to reflect Nestle's shares closed 1.35% lower at 87.51 francs on Wednesday. The original version incorrectly misstated the day as Thursday in the last sentence of the item.

data.title: Greek unemployment drops to 8.3% in April
data.description:
Greece's jobless rate stood at 8.3% in April, down from a downwardly revised 8.9% in March, data from statistics service ELSTAT showed on Friday.

Seasonally adjusted data showed 391,263 people were officially unemployed.

Unemployment impacted women more than men, at the rates of 10.8% and 6.3%, respectively.

The jobless rate for young people aged 15 to 24 years dropped to 20.4% in April from 24.2% in the same month in 2024, ELSTAT said.

data.title: Suzlon Energy's Consolidated Net Profit Sees Surge in Fiscal Q4; Shares Jump 10% Trade
data.description:
Suzlon Energy's saw a surge in its consolidated attributable net profit to 11.8 billion Indian rupees in the fiscal fourth quarter ended March 31, against 2.54 billion rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share climbed to 0.86 rupees compared with the year-ago figure of 0.19 rupees, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges by the wind energy company.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q4 also increased to 37.7 billion rupees from 21.8 billion rupees a year earlier.

The company's shares were up 10% in recent trade.
